ddls:
CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry (e.g., Finance, Healthcare, Technology) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_roles" (
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job role */,
  "job_role_name" TEXT /* Name of the job role (e.g., Software Engineer, Data Analyst, Marketing Manager) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the job role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., London, Manchester, Birmingham) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region in which the location is situated (e.g., North, South, Midlands) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary range */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Description of the salary range (e.g., < £18,031, ≥ £18,031 and < £24,057) */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the salary range */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of employees in the salary range */,
  "percentage_2018" REAL /* Percentage of employees in the salary range in 2018 */,
  "difference_with_previous_year" REAL /* Difference in percentage with the previous year */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the industries table */,
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the job_roles table */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the locations table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salaries_industry_id FOREIGN KEY ("industry_id") REFERENCES industries ("industry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salaries_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salaries_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary trend */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Description of the salary range (e.g., < £18,031, ≥ £18,031 and < £24,057) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the salary trend is recorded */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of employees in the salary range for the given year */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the industries table */,
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the job_roles table */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the locations table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_trends_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_trends_industry_id FOREIGN KEY ("industry_id") REFERENCES industries ("industry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_trends_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, viewer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the user's password for secure storage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activity" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user activity */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date on which the activity occurred */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity performed (e.g., data access, data export) */,
  "data_exported" INTEGER /* Indicates whether data was exported during the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_activity_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
